name: Personal Travel Concierge
description: AI travel agent managing a frequent business traveler's preferences, loyalty accounts, and trip history over a year. Tests preference drift, itinerary changes, and loyalty tier updates.

turns.content: I travel about 3-4 times a month for work. Always flying out of Chicago O'Hare, terminal preference is the United terminal.
Profile: Chicago O'Hare home base, United terminal preferred.
My United MileagePlus number is MP-4471-38829. I'm currently Gold status.
United MileagePlus: MP-4471-38829, Gold status.
Hotel preference: Marriott properties, always request a high floor corner room, away from the elevator. Bonvoy number is 7723-998-1144.
Hotel: Marriott preferred, high floor corner room away from elevator. Bonvoy 7723-998-1144.
I always want an aisle seat, never a window. And I need to be back by Thursday evenings when school is in session for my kid's Friday games.
Seat: always aisle. Travel constraint: home by Thursday evening during school year.
Add Global Entry to my profile — number is GE-7829344. Makes TSA much faster.
Global Entry saved: GE-7829344.
I've started preferring Hyatt over Marriott in New York. The Park Hyatt Midtown is my go-to there.
New York exception: Park Hyatt Midtown preferred over Marriott.
I had a bad experience with United on a flight to Boston — they lost my bag. Now I always carry on when going to Boston specifically.
Boston rule: carry-on only. No checked bags. (Lost bag incident with United.)
I hit 1K on United — upgraded to 1K status. Been flying a lot.
United status upgraded: Gold → 1K. MileagePlus MP-4471-38829.
I'm starting a monthly client trip to Austin. Preferred hotel there is the Fairmont.
Austin route added: monthly cadence, Fairmont Austin preferred.
Also — I prefer to land by 8 PM on any travel day. Gets too disruptive to the family if I'm landing late.
New preference: land by 8 PM on travel days whenever possible.
I moved to the western suburbs. Midway is now closer than O'Hare. I'll be flying out of Midway for short-haul and keeping O'Hare for longer routes.
Airport preference updated: Midway (MDW) for short-haul, O'Hare (ORD) for long-haul. Old default was O'Hare only.
I added an AmEx Platinum card — it gives lounge access. Use that for Centurion Lounges at airports.
AmEx Platinum added for Centurion Lounge access.
Bonvoy number is same — 7723-998-1144 — but I hit Platinum this year.
Bonvoy status updated: Platinum Elite. Number unchanged.
The Austin trips are going monthly but I actually prefer the Westin now — the Fairmont service has declined.
Austin hotel preference updated: Fairmont → Westin Austin Downtown.
Moved my Thursday-home constraint to apply year-round now, not just school year. My schedule is better that way.
Travel constraint updated: home by Thursday evening — now year-round, not school year only.
I've also started flying Southwest from Midway on certain short routes because of their open boarding. Add that as an option.
Southwest added as option for short-haul from Midway (open boarding appeal).

queries.query: What is my United MileagePlus status?
queries.expected_answer: 1K status. Started as Gold, upgraded after reaching 1,000 qualifying flights.
queries.trap: Gold established in session 1. 1K achieved in session 3. Must return current status.

queries.query: Which airport should I fly out of for a short trip to Nashville?
queries.expected_answer: Midway (MDW) — now preferred for short-haul routes since the move to the western suburbs.
queries.trap: O'Hare was the original home airport. Session 4 introduces the split: Midway for short-haul, O'Hare for long-haul.

queries.query: Where should I stay on my next Austin trip?
queries.expected_answer: Westin Austin Downtown. The Fairmont was previously preferred but switched due to declined service.
queries.trap: Fairmont set as Austin preference in session 3. Session 5 changes it to Westin. Naive systems return Fairmont.

queries.query: Is my Thursday-home constraint only during the school year?
queries.expected_answer: No — it was updated to apply year-round in session 5.
queries.trap: Session 1 explicitly says 'when school is in session.' Session 5 expands it to year-round.

queries.query: What hotel should I book for a trip to New York?
queries.expected_answer: Park Hyatt Midtown — exception to the general Marriott preference for New York trips.
queries.trap: General rule is Marriott. NY has a specific exception for Park Hyatt. System must apply the exception, not the general rule.

queries.query: What is my MileagePlus number?
What is my Bonvoy number and current status?
queries.expected_answer: Bonvoy 7723-998-1144, Platinum Elite status. Previously regular Bonvoy member.
queries.trap: Status updated from base to Platinum Elite in session 4. Number unchanged.

queries.query: I'm booking a trip to Boston — any special instructions?
queries.expected_answer: Carry-on only — no checked bags. United lost a bag on a Boston trip. Also: aisle seat, land by 8 PM, and Marriott if staying overnight.
queries.trap: Boston rule (carry-on only) from session 2 must be combined with general preferences. The specific rule overrides general luggage behavior.

queries.query: What seat type do I prefer?
queries.expected_answer: Aisle, never window.

queries.query: What is my Global Entry number?
Where was I staying in Austin before the switch?
queries.expected_answer: The Fairmont Austin — preference before service declined and I switched to the Westin.
queries.trap: Tests explicit historical recall of a superseded preference.

queries.query: Can I schedule a return flight on Friday afternoon?
queries.expected_answer: No. Standing rule: home by Thursday evening, year-round (updated from school-year-only).
queries.trap: Must apply the year-round version of the constraint, not the school-year-only version.

queries.query: What caused the switch away from checking bags for Boston trips?
queries.expected_answer: United lost a bag on a Boston flight. Now carry-on only for all Boston trips.

queries.query: Which airlines am I open to flying for short-haul out of Midway?
queries.expected_answer: Southwest (open boarding) in addition to any standard options. United for longer routes from O'Hare.
queries.trap: Southwest added in session 5 specifically for Midway short-haul. Must not apply it to long-haul routes.

queries.query: What hotel room preferences should be noted at check-in?
queries.expected_answer: High floor corner room, away from the elevator.

queries.query: What lounge access do I have at airports?
queries.expected_answer: United Club access via 1K status, plus Centurion Lounge access via AmEx Platinum card.
queries.trap: 1K status grants United Club. AmEx Platinum (session 4) adds Centurion. Both are currently active.

queries.query: What is my preferred landing time constraint?
queries.expected_answer: Land by 8 PM on any travel day whenever possible.

queries.query: When did the airport preference change and why?
queries.expected_answer: Session 4 — moved to western suburbs, making Midway closer. Now Midway for short-haul, O'Hare for long-haul.

queries.query: I'm booking a long-haul international trip from Chicago. Which airport?
queries.expected_answer: O'Hare (ORD) — kept as the preference for long-haul routes.
queries.trap: Session 4 splits airport preferences. Must apply the correct branch: long-haul → O'Hare.

queries.query: List all my exception rules that override the general preferences.
queries.expected_answer: New York: Park Hyatt instead of Marriott. Boston: carry-on only, no checked bags. Austin: Westin, not Fairmont. Short-haul from Midway: Southwest is acceptable. Thursday evening constraint: year-round (not just school year).
queries.trap: Requires collecting all city-specific and situation-specific overrides across sessions 2, 3, 5 — separate from general preferences.
